questions.question: What is the main goal of a Text Retrieval System (TRS)?
options: A) To rank documents by their content relevance
B) To extract specific information from unstructured documents
C) To classify documents into categories
D) To summarize long documents
questions.explanation: The main goal of a Text Retrieval System (TRS) is to rank documents by their content relevance to a given query.

questions.question: Which of the following is a key concept in the Term-Displacement Model?
options: A) The importance of the term frequency
B) The idea that the query is modified to match the document
C) The concept of relevance scores
D) The use of inverted indexes
questions.explanation: The Term-Displacement Model modifies the query to match the document, rather than the other way around.

questions.question: What is the main advantage of the Vector Space Model (VSM) over other IR models?
options: A) It can handle multiple queries simultaneously
B) It uses a mathematical model to represent text documents
C) It is based on the concept of term frequencies
D) It requires a large amount of training data
questions.explanation: The Vector Space Model (VSM) uses a mathematical model to represent text documents, making it a powerful tool for information retrieval.

questions.question: Which of the following is NOT a characteristic of the Classic Information Retrieval (CIR) model?
options: A) It relies on the concept of term frequencies
B) It uses a simple ranking algorithm
C) It is based on the idea of matching queries with documents
D) It requires a large amount of training data
questions.explanation: The Classic Information Retrieval (CIR) model is based on the idea of matching queries with documents, but it does not require a large amount of training data.

questions.question: What is the main difference between the Set Theoretic Model and the Term-Displacement Model?
options: A) The Set Theoretic Model uses a mathematical model, while the Term-Displacement Model modifies the query
B) The Term-Displacement Model uses a mathematical model, while the Set Theoretic Model modifies the query
C) The Set Theoretic Model is based on the concept of term frequencies, while the Term-Displacement Model is not
D) The Set Theoretic Model requires a large amount of training data, while the Term-Displacement Model does not
questions.explanation: The Set Theoretic Model uses a mathematical model to represent the intersection and union of sets, while the Term-Displacement Model modifies the query to match the document.
